AWS helps customers migrate their legacy.aws: AWS helps customers migrate their legacy Windows Server applications to the latest, supported versions of Windows Server on AWS, without any code changes. AWS offers the End-of-Support Migration Program (EMP) for Windows Server.
AWS helps customers migrate their legacy.aws-cn: Amazon Web Services helps customers migrate their legacy Windows Server applications to the latest, supported versions of Windows Server on Amazon Web Services, without any code changes. Amazon Web Services offers the End-of-Support Migration Program (EMP) for Windows Server.

AWS Migration Acceleration Program.aws-cn: Amazon Web Services Migration Acceleration Program

The AWS Migration Acceleration Program (.aws: The AWS Migration Acceleration Program (MAP) for Windows is designed to help organizations reach their migration goals faster with AWS services, best practices, tools, and incentives.
The AWS Migration Acceleration Program (.aws-cn: The Amazon Web Services Migration Acceleration Program (MAP) for Windows is designed to help organizations reach their migration goals faster with Amazon Web Services, best practices, tools, and incentives.

AWS has the concept of a Region, which i.aws: AWS has the concept of a Region, which is a physical location around the world where we cluster data centers. We call each group of logical data centers an Availability Zone. Each AWS Region consists of multiple, isolated, and physically separate AZ’s within a geographic area. Unlike other cloud providers, who often define a region as a single data center, the multiple AZ design of every AWS Region offers advantages for customers. Each AZ has independent power, cooling, and physical security and is connected via redundant, ultra-low-latency networks. AWS customers focused on high availability can design their applications to run in multiple AZ’s to achieve even greater fault-tolerance. AWS infrastructure Regions meet the highest levels of security, compliance, and data protection.
AWS has the concept of a Region, which i.aws-cn: Amazon Web Services China (Beijing) Region and Amazon Web Services China (Ningxia) Region are the two Amazon Web Services Regions located within China. To provide the best experience for customers in China and to comply with China’s legal and regulatory requirements, Amazon Web Services has collaborated with China local partners with proper telecom licenses for delivering cloud services. The service operator and provider for Amazon Web Services China (Beijing) Region based out of Beijing and adjacent areas is Beijing Sinnet Technology Co., Ltd. (Sinnet), and the service operator and provider for Amazon Web Services (Ningxia) Region based out of Ningxia is Ningxia Western Cloud Data Technology Co., Ltd. (NWCD).

It is a physical location around the wor.aws: It is a physical location around the world where AWS clusters data centers.

The Basic support plan is included for a.aws: The Basic support plan is included for all AWS customers. This plan includes 24/7 access to customer service, documentation, whitepapers, and access to support forums. To ensure that you’re following best practices to increase performance and improve security, you have access to seven core Trusted Advisor checks. You also have a personalized view of your AWS service’s health, and access to alerts when your resources are impacted.
The Basic support plan is included for a.aws-cn: The Basic support plan is included for all Amazon Web Services customers. This plan includes 24/7 access to customer service, documentation, whitepapers, and access to support forums. To ensure that you’re following best practices to increase performance and improve security, you have access to seven core Trusted Advisor checks. You also have a personalized view of your Amazon Web Services service’s health, and access to alerts when your resources are impacted.

strings.Supports one primary contact with a Clou: Supports one primary contact with a Cloud Support associate during business hours, with a response time of 12 hours or less for system issues.

AWS Regions & Availability Zones.aws-cn: Amazon Web Services Regions & Availability Zones

Get started creating an estimate by givi.aws: Get started creating an estimate by giving your estimate a name and selecting a Region. Your estimate must have a Region, but you can create groups in your estimate that have different Regions. Prices and availability of AWS services vary by Region. You can always change the name of your estimate or groups. As long as your estimate or groups have no services in them, you can change the Region.
Get started creating an estimate by givi.aws-cn: Get started creating an estimate by giving your estimate a name and selecting a Region. Your estimate must have a Region, but you can create groups in your estimate that have different Regions. Prices and availability of Amazon Web Services services vary by Region. You can always change the name of your estimate or groups. As long as your estimate or groups have no services in them, you can change the Region.

strings.Groups are containers that you add servi: Groups are containers that you add services and other groups to.
strings.Groups make it easy to clone multiple se: Groups make it easy to clone multiple service configurations at a time. You can use them to organize your estimate, such as providing estimates by cost center, department, or product architecture.

AWS Support provides a mix of tools and.aws: AWS Support provides a mix of tools and technology, people, and programs designed to proactively help you optimize performance, lower costs, and innovate faster. We save time for your team by helping you to move faster in the cloud and focus on your core business.

Your data will be stored on AWS public s.aws: Your data will be stored on AWS public servers. The calculator will generate an obscure, but publicly accessible URL. Anyone with the public share link will be able to access your estimate.
Your data will be stored on AWS public s.aws-cn: Your data will be stored on Amazon Web Services public servers. The calculator will generate an obscure, but publicly accessible URL. Anyone with the public share link will be able to access your estimate.

Your estimate will be stored within AWS.aws: Your estimate will be stored within AWS infrastructure that is not connected to the Internet. The calculator will generate an obscure share link, which you can use to share your estimate with others. Anybody with access to this infrastructure who receives the share link will be able to read your estimate and re-share the URL with others. Do not enter sensitive information into your estimate.
Your estimate will be stored within AWS.aws-cn: Your estimate will be stored within Amazon Web Services infrastructure that is not connected to the Internet. The calculator will generate an obscure share link, which you can use to share your estimate with others. Anybody with access to this infrastructure who receives the share link will be able to read your estimate and re-share the URL with others. Do not enter sensitive information into your estimate.

AWS has the concept of a Region, which i.aws: AWS has the concept of a Region, which is a physical location around the world where we cluster data centers. We call each group of logical data centers an Availability Zone. Each AWS Region consists of multiple, isolated, and physically separate AZ’s within a geographic area. Unlike other cloud providers, who often define a region as a single data center, the multiple AZ design of every AWS Region offers advantages for customers. Each AZ has independent power, cooling, and physical security and is connected via redundant, ultra-low-latency networks. AWS customers focused on high availability can design their applications to run in multiple AZ’s to achieve even greater fault-tolerance. AWS infrastructure Regions meet the highest levels of security, compliance, and data protection. AWS Wavelength embeds AWS compute and storage services within 5G networks, providing mobile edge computing infrastructure for developing, deploying, and scaling ultra-low-latency applications. AWS Local Zones are a type of infrastructure deployment that places compute, storage, database, and other select AWS services close to large population and industry centers.
AWS has the concept of a Region, which i.aws-cn: Amazon Web Services China (Beijing) Region and Amazon Web Services China (Ningxia) Region are the two Amazon Web Services Regions located within China. To provide the best experience for customers in China and to comply with China’s legal and regulatory requirements, Amazon Web Services has collaborated with China local partners with proper telecom licenses for delivering cloud services. The service operator and provider for Amazon Web Services China (Beijing) Region based out of Beijing and adjacent areas is Beijing Sinnet Technology Co., Ltd. (Sinnet), and the service operator and provider for Amazon Web Services (Ningxia) Region based out of Ningxia is Ningxia Western Cloud Data Technology Co., Ltd. (NWCD).

Any discount applied to applicable services is based on your input and is for estimation purposes only.Discount applicability and the discount percentage will depend on contracted terms. Access to view marketing documents for AWS services will depend on the partition in which you are seeking information.

The AWS Pricing Calculator estimates ser.aws: The AWS Pricing Calculator estimates service costs based on a normalized monthly time frame. The Calculator assumes there are {0} hours in a month (({1} days * {2} hours) / {3} months in a year), which may be less or more than the actual hours in the current billing period.
The AWS Pricing Calculator estimates ser.aws-cn: The Amazon Pricing Calculator estimates service costs based on a normalized monthly time frame. The Calculator assumes there are {0} hours in a month (({1} days * {2} hours) / {3} months in a year), which may be less or more than the actual hours in the current billing period.

strings.Any discount applied to applicable servi: Any discount applied to applicable services is based on your input and is for estimation purposes only. Discount applicability and the discount percentage will depend on contracted terms.
strings.Is the discount included in the estimate: Is the discount included in the estimates created using the AWS Pricing Calculator?
strings.All estimates created using the calculat: All estimates created using the calculator include the discount percentage you entered for creation of the estimate.
strings.The AWS Pricing Calculator is an estimat: The AWS Pricing Calculator is an estimation tool that provides an approximate cost of using AWS services based on the usage parameters that you specify. The AWS Pricing Calculator is not a quote tool, and does not guarantee the cost for your actual use of AWS services. The cost estimated by the AWS Pricing Calculator may vary from your actual costs for a number of reasons. Common reasons the estimate may be different from your actual cost include:
strings.If you are concerned about these potenti: If you are concerned about these potential variations, consider using AWS Budgets. AWS Budgets gives you the ability to set custom budgets that alert you when your costs or usage exceed (or are forecasted to exceed) your budgeted amount. You could also define actions you want to take in your account when a budget exceeds its threshold (actual or forecasted amounts).
strings.Your actual cost will be based on your a: Your actual cost will be based on your actual use of the services, not the Calculator. The accuracy of your inputs into the Calculator affects the accuracy of your actual costs. For example,
strings.If you estimate you will use a certain n: If you estimate you will use a certain number of EC2 instances, but you actually use more, you will be billed for your actual utilization.
strings.If you use AWS services that you did not: If you use AWS services that you did not include in your estimate, then your estimate will differ from your actual bill.
strings.If you over or under estimate Data Trans: If you over- or under-estimate Data Transfer inputs or outputs for AWS services, your actual bill may vary.
strings.If you estimate your service use based o: If you estimate your service use based on purchasing a 3-year reservation for a service, but you buy a 1-year reservation, your monthly and upfront prices will vary from the estimate.
strings.The prices for AWS services vary between: The prices for AWS services vary between Regions. Using different Region than the one used in the Calculator will affect the accuracy of the results.
strings.AWS offers pay-as-you-go pricing for mos: AWS offers pay-as-you-go pricing for most services and prices may change over time. If you buy services On Demand, your bill may be less or more than estimated, based on the current On Demand service rates. For certain services, you may buy a 1 or 3 year reservation to lock in the price as of the date of your reservation.
strings.The Calculator does not include any taxe: The Calculator does not include any taxes that may be applied to your purchase of the services.
strings.The Calculator assumes {0} hours in a mo: The Calculator assumes {0} hours in a month, ({1} days in a year x {2} hours in a day / {3} months in year). The estimated, first twelve month’s total is calculated by (({4} x monthly total cost) + upfront total costs). The Calculator does not account for leap years, which adds one day.
strings.The Calculator only shows your estimated: The Calculator only shows your estimated totals up to the first {0} months of utilization. If you estimate costs with a {1} year, partial upfront pricing strategy, you will only see estimated costs adjusted for the first {2} months of the {3} year reservation.
strings.Free tier, promotional credits and disco: Free tier, promotional credits and discounts
strings.The Calculator does not account for free: The Calculator does not account for free tier pricing, promotional credits, or other discounts. You may be eligible for additional discounts that are not captured in the estimate. Contact sales for more information.
strings.AWS bills on a monthly basis. If your ut: AWS bills on a monthly basis. If your utilization starts mid-month, you will only see a portion of an actual month’s complete costs.
strings.The Calculator performs mathematical rou: The Calculator performs mathematical rounding of pricing data. For details on how the Calculator rounds, refer to the pricing math contained in the “Show calculation” section of each service configuration.
strings.You may qualify for volume discounts bas: You may qualify for volume discounts based on your current use of AWS services, outside of what you estimate in the AWS Pricing Calculator. The Calculator cannot account for usage costs that it can’t see.
strings.For example, if you already use {0} TB p: For example, if you already use {0} TB per month of Amazon S3 Standard storage, you pay ${1} per GB. If you use the Calculator to estimate an additional {2} TB per month, the Calculator will assume this is the first {3} TB per month which is costed higher at ${4} per GB. Your cumulative actual spend on {5} TB per month on Amazon S3 would be less than the estimated additional cost to your current spend.

Some AWS services use per second billing.aws: Some AWS services use per second billing. The Calculator does not account for per second billing options.
Some AWS services use per second billing.aws-cn: Some services of Amazon Web Services use per second billing. The Calculator does not account for per second billing options.Some services of Amazon Web Services use per second billing. The Calculator does not account for per second billing options.

The Calculator does not account for thir.aws: The Calculator does not account for third-party licensing fees, such as software solutions deployed from AWS Marketplace.
The Calculator does not account for thir.aws-cn: The Calculator does not account for third-party licensing fees, such as software solutions deployed from Amazon Web Services Marketplace.

strings.The Calculator does not account for thir: The Calculator does not account for third-party licensing fees, such as software solutions deployed from Amazon Web Services Marketplace.
strings.The Calculator estimates in U.S. dollars: The Calculator estimates in U.S. dollars. Global exchange rates vary over time. If you translate your estimate to a different currency based on the current exchange, changes in the exchange rate will affect the estimate.
strings.The Amazon Pricing Calculator is an esti: The Amazon Pricing Calculator is an estimation tool that provides an approximate cost of using Amazon Web Services based on the usage parameters that you specify. The Amazon Pricing Calculator is not a quote tool, and does not guarantee the cost for your actual use of Amazon Web Services. The cost estimated by the Amazon Pricing Calculator may vary from your actual costs for a number of reasons. Common reasons the estimate may be different from your actual cost include:
strings.If you are concerned about these potenti: If you are concerned about these potential variations, consider using Amazon Budgets. Amazon Budgets gives you the ability to set custom budgets that alert you when your costs or usage exceed (or are forecasted to exceed) your budgeted amount. You could also define actions you want to take in your account when a budget exceeds its threshold (actual or forecasted amounts).
strings.If you use services of Amazon Web Servic: If you use services of Amazon Web Services that you did not include in your estimate, then your estimate will differ from your actual bill.
strings.If you over or under estimate Data Trans: If you over or under estimate Data Transfer inputs or outputs for services of Amazon Web Services, your actual bill may vary.
strings.The prices for Amazon Web Services vary: The prices for Amazon Web Services vary between Regions. Using different Region than the one used in the Calculator will affect the accuracy of the results.
strings.Amazon Web Services offers pay-as-you-go: Amazon Web Services offers pay-as-you-go pricing for most services and prices may change over time. If you buy services On Demand, your bill may be less or more than estimated, based on the current On Demand service rates. For certain services, you may buy a 1 or 3 year reservation to lock in the price as of the date of your reservation.
strings.The Calculator assumes {0} hours in a mo: The Calculator assumes {0} hours in a month, ({1} days in a year x {2} hours in a day / {3} months in year). The estimated, first twelve month’s total is calculated by {4} x total monthly cost + total upfront cost. The Calculator does not account for leap years, which adds one day.
strings.The Calculator only shows your estimated: The Calculator only shows your estimated totals up to the first {0} months. If you estimate costs with a {1} year, partial upfront pricing strategy, you will only see estimated costs adjusted for the first {2} months of the {3} year reservation.
strings.The Calculator does not account for free: The Calculator does not account for free tier pricing, promotional credits, or other discounts. You may be eligible for additional discounts that are not captured in the estimate. Contact Us for more information.
strings.Amazon Web Services bills on a monthly b: Amazon Web Services bills on a monthly basis. If your utilization starts mid-month, you will only see a portion of an actual month’s complete costs.
strings.You may qualify for volume discounts bas: You may qualify for volume discounts based on your current use of Amazon Web Services, outside of what you estimate in the Amazon Pricing Calculator. The Calculator does not account for usage that you do not include in the estimate.
strings.For example, if you already use {0} TB p: For example, if you already use {0} TB per month of Amazon S3 Standard storage, you pay ¥{1} per GB. If you use the Calculator to estimate an additional {2} TB per month, the Calculator will assume this is the first {3} TB per month which is costed higher at ¥{4} per GB. Your cumulative actual spend on {5} TB per month on Amazon S3 would be less than the estimated additional cost to your current spend.
strings.Some services of Amazon Web Services use: Some services of Amazon Web Services use per second billing. The Calculator does not account for per second billing options.
strings.The Calculator estimates in Chinese Yuan: The Calculator estimates in Chinese Yuan Renminbi ([currency]). Global exchange rates vary over time. If you translate your estimate to a different currency based on the current exchange, changes in the exchange rate will affect the estimate.
strings.You may qualify for volume discounts bas: You may qualify for volume discounts based on your current use of AWS services, outside of what you estimate in the AWS Pricing Calculator. The Calculator does not account for usage that you do not include in the estimate.
strings.Some instances have additional costs whe: Some instances have additional costs when it comes to burst capacity, (e.g., T2 and T3 unlimited instances). You accrue burst credits when running burstable instances, which you can use toward your bill, but if you exhaust those burst credits, you would need to pay additional costs for those overages. For more information on burstable instances,
strings.In order to deliver a cost-optimized pri: In order to deliver a cost-optimized pricing strategy, the Calculator performs a break-even analysis for your specified instance and workload. The analysis evaluates the cost for both on-demand and reserved instance scenarios and delivers a recommendation for either pricing model or a combination of the two for the most cost-effective solution.
strings.A workload is only as accurate as it is: A workload is only as accurate as it is predictive of your actual workload. For example, if you use more than you estimate, you will be billed for more. If you use less, you will be billed for less.
strings.The break-even analysis is based upon a: The break-even analysis is based upon a {0} hour month. Your actual month may have a different break-even point, and every instance has a different break-even point based on its own On Demand and Reserve Instance prices.
strings.Daily: There is an uneven distribution o: Daily: There is an uneven distribution of days in a month. If you only run instances on Monday, we normalize how many Mondays are in a month. The actual may be {0} or {1} for the current month, but we average this to {2} Mondays in a month. (Example math: {3} hours in a month / ({4} hours in a day * {5} days in a week) = {6} occurrences of a specific day in a month.)
strings.Weekly: The Calculator assumes there are: Weekly: The Calculator assumes there are {0} weeks in a month.
strings.Monthly: The Calculator assumes there ar: Monthly: The Calculator assumes there are {0} hours in a month.
strings.Custom: The Calculator assumes that all: Custom: The Calculator assumes that all instances described in the custom workload are simultaneously running. When the Calculator performs a break-even analysis for a cost-optimized scenario, it does not know when the various custom workload instances are running, simultaneously or serially. If the instances are not running simultaneously, it may be cheaper to buy more reserved instances.
strings.With Spot instances, you pay the Spot pr: With Spot instances, you pay the Spot price that's in effect for the time period your instances are running. The Spot pricing strategy assumes a historical average discount for the instance type you select, however, historical values may not be predictive of the actual discount at the time of your utilization. You may adjust the assumed discount as best fits your estimation model, however, the assumed discount is not a guarantee for your actual cost.
strings.For the Amazon Elastic Block Storage (EB: For the Amazon Elastic Block Storage (EBS) estimate, the calculator assumes that your EBS snapshots are retained for one month, and then subsequently deleted.
strings.Custom: The Calculator assumes that all: Custom: The Calculator assumes that all instances described in the custom workload are simultaneously running. When the Calculator performs a break-even analysis for a cost-optimized scenario, it does not know when the various custom workload instances are running, simultaneously or serially. If the instances are not running simultaneously, it may be cheaper to buy more reserved instances. To learn more, Contact Us.
strings.With Spot instances, you pay the Spot pr: With Spot instances, you pay the Spot price that's in effect for the time period your instances are running. You may adjust the assumed discount as best fits your estimation model. However, the assumed discount is not a guarantee for your actual cost.
